mission.title: n8n: Better authentication with service providers
mission.description: Better authentication with service providers. Requested by 7+ users.

steps.title: Check current n8n deployment
steps.description:
Verify your n8n version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
helm list -n n8n 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working n8n installation.

steps.title: Review n8n configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant n8n configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
kubectl get configmap -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=n8n
```
From what I could understand, all of the credentials need to be manually created and inserted by the user on the CREDENTIALS menu.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Better authentication with service providers
steps.description:
Adds support for basic Oauth2 support extendable to provider specific credentials.

Uses `rest/oauth2-credential/callback` end-point as a callback URL. The users have to use this to register as allowed callback URL with the providers.

I'm opening this PR for starting a discussion around what's needed to merge this branch into master.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
kubectl get events -n n8n --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Better authentication with service providers" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Adds support for basic Oauth2 support extendable to provider specific credentials.

Uses `rest/oauth2-credential/callback` end-point as a callback URL. The users have to use this to register as allowed callback URL with the providers.

I'm opening this PR for starting a discussion around what's needed to merge this branch into master.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with n8n installed or the issue environment reproducible.
